prompt: A lone mountain hiker in dense fog on a steep slope, wearing heavy waterproof gear and a weathered backpack. The hiker is caught mid-step, looking down at the ground directly beneath their boots to find the direction of descent—they cannot see the valley below through the thick mist. Their posture is lean, forward-focused, deliberate. The ground beneath them is a complex rocky scree field with subtle elevation changes; their boots press into loose stone. The slope descends visibly but gradually curves away into white fog. Overcast daylight. The mood is focused, patient, methodical—the hiker is feeling their way downward step by step, relying only on the immediate tactile feedback of the terrain under their feet. No maps, no compass visible. No labels, no text, no annotations. Shot from a mid-distance angle that shows both the hiker's body and the immediate terrain texture. Cinematic, volumetric mist, natural lighting, photographic realism. Negative prompt: No diagrams, no flowcharts, no arrows, no labels, no glowing effects, no abstract visualizations, no charts, no equations, no text overlays, no UI elements, no digital graphics, no floating annotations, no contour lines, no graphs. Keep it photorealistic and grounded.
metaphor: A hiker descends a foggy slope by sensing ground beneath their feet. Each step follows the immediate slope direction. Iterative downward movement guided only by local feedback.
